chapter_title.en: The Chapter of Condolences and Grief During Calamities, Visiting Graves, Lamentation and Mourning Gatherings

parts.text: وَقَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «إِنَّ اَلنَّبِيَّ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ
آلِهِ حِينَ جَاءَتْهُ وَفَاةُ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ أَبِي طَالِبٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ
زَيْدِ بْنِ حَارِثَةَ كَانَ إِذَا دَخَلَ بَيْتَهُ كَثُرَ بُكَاؤُهُ عَلَيْهِمَا جِدّاً وَ

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.527 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"When The Prophet (sw) received news of the deaths of Ja'far ibn Abi Talib (as) and Zayd ibn Harithah, he would weep intensely when he entered his house and would say,
'They used to converse with me and bring me comfort, and now they are both gone.'"
